title: Whiskey Peak Welcomes the Straw Hats — Part 6
description: On pages 16–18, whiskey peak welcomes the straw hats — part 6 as after facing the Grand Line’s bizarre weather, the crew reaches Whiskey Peak and is greeted with suspicious enthusiasm.
scene: Whiskey Peak Welcomes the Straw Hats — Part 6. After facing the Grand Line’s bizarre weather, the crew reaches Whiskey Peak and is greeted with suspicious enthusiasm. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Huh? Not only are there no monsters, but there're people greeting us.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: long live the brave heroes of the sea!

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: T-There's a bunch of lovely ladies too!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Looks like pirates really are the people's heroes!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: You may be a little shocked at the welcome, but this is whiskey peak, a town of liquor and music.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: If it's fine liquor you want, we have an ocean's worth.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: So please, I cordially invite you to our banquet,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: And hope that you may entread-

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: entreat us with tales of your adventures.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Ah, by the way, how long will it take for this island's log to be recorded?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Please put aside thinking of such weary matters for now and focus on resting from your journey!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: And let us sing to our heart's content for these adventures!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: The sun sets on whiskey peak.

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on whiskey peak welcomes the straw hats — part 6. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
